articles.title: Call for Papers: IEEE Brain Special Issue
articles.content: In a unique interdisciplinary collaboration with the IEEE’s Society on Social Implications of Technology (SSIT) and IEEE Brain, J-FLEX is joining forces to explore both the technology of the Internet-of-Medical-Things (IoMT) solutions and medical wearables/implantables.
articles.author: ieeebrain

articles.title: IEEE Brain Joins the American Brain Coalition
articles.content: IEEE Brain is pleased to announce its acceptance as a nonprofit member of the American Brain Coalition (ABC), a prestigious alliance of over 150 organizations dedicated to advancing brain research, advocacy, and improving treatments for individuals affected by brain conditions. The ABC Board has enthusiastically welcomed IEEE Brain into its network, reinforcing a shared commitment to fostering innovation and collaboration ...

The multiple scales of astrocytic functional units
articles.content:
Nature Neuroscience, Published online: 27 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41593-026-02308-x

Astrocytes modulate complex brain functions. These ubiquitous glial cells constitute a multilayered system of functional units that operate across multiple spatial scales, thereby increasing the degrees of freedom in brain information processing.
articles.author: Christian Henneberger

articles.title: Design an anthropomorphic dexterous hand for expressive piano performance
articles.content: BackgroundExpressive piano performance poses extreme challenges for robotic manipulation, necessitating high-speed repetitive impacts, substantial force output, and coordinated multi-joint control under stringent dynamic constraints. However, existing robotic systems exhibit significant limitations in replicating human-level dexterity, as well as achievable motion speed and force output. This work presents a data-driven, bio-inspired dexterous robotic hand designed specifically for high-fidelity piano performance.MethodsWe first extract kinematic primitives and stable inter-joint coupling patterns from large-scale motion capture data of professional pianists. These human motion priors are directly embedded into the mechanical architecture through morphological coupling and actuator allocation. Actuator selection is further guided by empirically measured human peak velocities and force profiles from biomechanics literature, ensuring sufficient bandwidth for high-speed repetitive motion and adequate force transmission.ResultsExperimental results demonstrate that the proposed hand replicates human-like joint coordination, achieves peak joint velocities of 53.88 rad/s, and provides sufficient fingertip force for authentic piano interaction. As a demonstration of its capabilities, the hand successfully performs a Grade 7 piano piece, Croatian Rhapsody, illustrating its potential for expressive musical performance.ConclusionThis research establishes a principled pathway from human motion statistics to embodied robotic intelligence, providing a high-performance hardware foundation for autonomous musical performance.
articles.author: Hongtao Wang

articles.title: Monthly Updates [February]
articles.content:
INFO

These update threads only contain major updates. If you're interested in seeing all minor changes you can follow our Commits Page on GitHub or Updates Channel in Discord.

Wiki Updates

  • Our site's default search is now Exact Match / 2, which is a lot more accurate. You can toggle between both exact and fuzzy. TY to Zenith for doing this.

  • Added Note Tooltips / 2 that can be viewed simply by hovering over them. TY to Bread for doing this.

  • You can now navigate through Multiple Result Pages in a single search, page titles are now included in searches, and there is a blur effect behind the window. TY to Bread.

  • Added an Index Toggle / 2 to view only the indexes on a page, similar to the star toggle. TY to Zenith.

  • Fixed multiple mobile issues, including search not updating when typing; the navigation bar will now show immediately when scrolling up, instead of having to go all the way up. TY to Bread.

  • Added Monochrome / 2 as an option to the color picker. TY to Zenith.

  • Added a section for Xbox Homebrew in Gaming Tools.

  • Added a section for Undertale / Deltarune in Gaming Tools. TY to Nattakit, Eitan, Chara, and Clara for making this.

  • Added a section for RimWorld Tools in Gaming Tools. TY to Litekin for making this.

  • Added a section for Aerospace Engineering under Educational. TY to Yeet + Manu for making this.

  • Added a section for Indie Browser Games + Starred Nitrome.

  • Added a section for Unblocked Browser Games + Starred Selenite.

  • Added a section for HRT / Trans Health under the Health section. TY to April + Pluey for making this.

Stars Added ⭐

  • Starred Hydra in Game Launchers. Has plugin-based downloads, torrents, RD + TorBox, good customization, achievements, friends list, playtime monitor, cloud saves, etc. ⁠

  • Starred SpotiFLAC-Mobile in Android Audio. Open-source download app with support for multiple sites.

  • Starred both ElyPrismLauncher + Freesm Launcher in MC Launchers. Both are feature-rich forks of Prism that stood out more than other forks we tried.

  • Starred Gemini in AI Chatbots. Gemini 3 Flash works well now and is ranked highly on LMArena.

  • Starred Morphe in Android YouTube + Android YouTube Music. Ad-free YouTube patcher / ReVanced alternative.

  • Starred XDMovies + The One Piece in Video Downloading. Both have big libraries + fast hosts.

  • Starred mpvEx in Android Video Players. Feature-rich mpv fork, improved UI, well-maintained.

  • Starred Virgil Game Search in Game Download. Custom multi-site game download search engine; uses only trusted sources.

  • Starred Dan Store in Freeware Sites. Web-based Microsoft Store explorer; bypasses the need for the Microsoft Store app.

  • Starred KoReader in Ebook Readers. Open-source, highly customizable, plugin support; very good for e-readers.

  • Starred Nothing To Watch in Movie Recommendation Tools. Interactive discovery visualization; a fun/unique way to find something to watch.

  • Starred Ekvis in Geography. Well-maintained and useful geography learning site; covers 220+ countries, flags, territories, etc.

  • Starred Slope Plus in Browser Games. Updated version of Slope; has extra features, game modes, and an active custom map community.

  • Starred GeminiGen AI in Image + Video Gen. Gives unlimited Nano Banana Pro images, and unlimited Sora 2, Veo 3.1, and Grok videos.

  • Starred Perchance Image Gen. Unlimited, no sign-up required, and has improved a lot quality-wise.

  • Starred Flow-Fi in Lo-Fi Radio. Has lots of options for stations and video backgrounds; higher-quality than most lo-fi sites we're sent.

  • Starred StreetComplete in the Navigation section. Helps fill in missing OpenStreetMap data by completing quests.

  • Starred Flash Arch, Kongregate, & Crazy Games in Browser Games. After going through this section, these three seemed to stand out the most, but if we missed any other good ones, feel free to let us know. We did also consider AddictingGames + Poki, but were too unsure on both.

Things Removed

  • Removed BATO.to as their staff has been threatened / 2, and they've been forced to shut down the site.

  • Removed EeveeSpotifyReborn as it's been archived.

  • Removed MangaPark as their site is redirecting to ads now.
